essays:
$$\text{MATRIX MULTIPLICATION:}$$
$$\begin{bmatrix} 1 & 2 \\ 3 & 4 \end{bmatrix} \times \begin{bmatrix} 5 & 6 \\ 7 & 8 \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} 19 & 22 \\ 43 & 50 \end{bmatrix}$$
$$\text{EACH ENTRY IN THE RESULT COMES FROM A ROW OF THE FIRST MATRIX DOTTED}$$
$$\text{WITH A COLUMN OF THE SECOND. FOR EXAMPLE, } 19 = 1*5 + 2*7, \text{ AND } 50 =$$
$$3*6 + 4*8.$$

$\text{CONVOLUTION (FILTER APPLIED TO IMAGE PATCH):}$
$[1 \;\; 0] \quad [4 \;\; 5]$
$[0 \;\; -1] * [6 \;\; 7] = -3$
$\text{THE FILTER IS LAID DIRECTLY OVER THE PATCH, EACH PAIR OF OVERLAPPING}$
$\text{CELLS IS MULTIPLIED, AND ALL FOUR PRODUCTS ARE SUMMED: } (1*4)+(0*5)+$
$(0*6)+(-1*7) = 4 - 7 = -3.$

```latex
\begin{verbatim}
DEF MULTIPLICATION_TABLE(N):
    TABLE = []
    FOR I IN RANGE(1, N + 1):
        ROW = []
        FOR J IN RANGE(1, N + 1):
            ROW.APPEND(I * J)
\end{verbatim}
```
